summary:
Continuous hypersensitivity suggests possible crack extension near/past dental pulp necessitating further investigation; mild cases may respond well initially to \(Z\)O\(E\) application but persistent symptoms warrant considering options including direct pulp capping or potentially full pulpectomy based on severity findings upon examination.
The development of continuous hypersensitivity indicates an urgent need beyond routine monitoring due to possibilities ranging from structural integrity concerns involving nearby/tooth-pulp proximity down to material sensitivities which demand immediate evaluation via diagnostic procedures before deciding between non-invasive palliative steps vs definitive surgical corrections if indicated clinically. } Agent1 summarized their reasoning effectively within two concise sentences while focusing solely on critical aspects related directly addressing why continued hypersensitivity requires active management over mere observation alone. The summary highlights both differential diagnoses leading up to appropriate next actions needed when faced with unrelenting pain post-restoration work. 

Key Points Summarized:
- Persistent hypersensitivity signals deeper problems likely linked structurally around/into root canal system
- Immediate assessment crucial using diagnostics then tailored therapy chosen according whether issue superficial enough treated conservatively ($ZO_E$), moderate indicating advanced care like pulp protection techniques, severe demanding radical extraction methods
